name: Metropolitan Teachers
description_short: Metropolitan Teachers Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving educators and their families in Nashville, TN with loans, savings accounts, and financial services.
description_long: Metropolitan Teachers Credit Union was founded in the 1950s by eight educators seeking to address financial challenges faced by African American teachers. The organization evolved through leadership transitions, obtaining a State Charter and achieving million-dollar status before modernization efforts under Dr. Eunice P. Grisby. Today, under Samuel Kirk's direction, MTCU continues to serve its core mission of supporting the educator community. The credit union operates from its main office in Nashville and maintains a focus on competitive services and member growth. Metropolitan Teachers Credit Union offers a range of traditional banking and lending products. Members can access savings accounts, online account access, auto loans (as low as 5.00% APR as of September 2023), summer loans (as low as 12.00% APR), and home repair loans. The credit union also provides financial calculators and a streamlined online application process for membership and loan applications. These services are designed to help members build financial stability and achieve their financial goals. The credit union distinguishes itself through its specialized focus on serving employees of the Metropolitan Nashville Board of Education, their immediate family members, and small businesses within a twenty-five mile radius. This membership limitation creates a tight-knit community of educators and their networks. The organization has deep historical roots in the African American educator community and emphasizes personal service—members can visit the physical location or complete applications online and receive direct assistance from staff. Metropolitan Teachers Credit Union operates with limited hours (2:00 p.m. to 5:00 p.m. Monday-Friday) and serves a geographically specific membership base. While the website shows competitive rates on auto loans, home repair loan rates require calling the credit union directly. The organization's accessibility is limited to those who meet specific membership criteria and may be geographically restricted to the Nashville area. For those who qualify, it represents a community-focused alternative to larger financial institutions.
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting at 5.00% APR
Specialized focus on serving educators and their families with tailored financial services
Membership eligibility extends to immediate family and small businesses within 25-mile radius
Online account enrollment and loan application process available
Physical location with direct staff assistance at 1605 Jefferson St, Nashville
Deep historical roots serving the African American educator community since the 1950s
Multiple loan products including auto, home repair, and summer loans
cons: Limited operating hours (2:00 p.m. to 5:00 p.m. Monday-Friday only) restrict accessibility
Membership restricted to specific categories (MNBOE employees, family, local businesses) — not open to general public
Home repair loan rates not published on website, requiring phone call for current rates
Single physical location in Nashville limits convenience for members outside immediate area
Website content and rates last updated September 2023 — current rates and offerings may differ
best_for: Employees of Metropolitan Nashville Board of Education seeking community-focused banking
Educators and their immediate family members wanting competitive auto and personal loan rates
Small business owners within 25 miles of Nashville seeking credit union services
Members prioritizing in-person service and community connection over 24/7 digital banking
diagnosis: Metropolitan Teachers Credit Union is best for educators employed by or connected to the Metropolitan Nashville Board of Education who value community-focused banking and competitive loan rates. The main caveat is strict membership eligibility requirements and limited operating hours that restrict accessibility compared to mainstream banks or online-first financial institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
